seoTitle: How to Set Up Inventory in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to set up inventory in QuickBooks Online step by step, from creating items to tracking stock levels and recording purchases.
intro: Getting inventory set up correctly from the start saves headaches later when you're trying to track stock and cost of goods sold. This guide walks through the key steps for setting up inventory in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Turn on inventory tracking
steps.body: Enable inventory tracking in your account settings if it isn't already active.

steps.heading: Create an inventory item
steps.body: Set up a new item as an inventory type, entering details like cost, sales price, and starting quantity.

steps.heading: Assign the right accounts
steps.body: Link the item to the correct income, expense, and asset accounts so transactions post correctly.

steps.heading: Track stock as you buy and sell
steps.body: Use purchase orders and sales forms tied to the item so quantities update automatically as you transact.

faq.question: Do I need a specific QuickBooks Online plan for inventory tracking?
faq.answer: Inventory tracking is typically available on higher-tier plans, so confirm your subscription supports it.

faq.question: What's the difference between inventory and non-inventory items?
faq.answer: Inventory items track quantity on hand and cost of goods sold, while non-inventory items don't track stock levels.

faq.question: Can I set a reorder point for inventory items?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set a reorder point so QuickBooks flags when stock is running low.

faq.question: How does QuickBooks calculate cost of goods sold for inventory?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online typically uses an average cost method to calculate cost of goods sold as inventory items are sold.
